hypothesis: After the accepted low-score plain risk-on sizing lift, the next alpha decision should come from a three-window sleeve/sector/multiplier map rather than another local threshold or sector-size tweak.
why_not_llm_soft_ranking: LLM soft-ranking remains high-upside but production-aligned ranking-eligible replay samples are still too thin; this audit looked for deterministic allocation alpha instead.

parameters.single_causal_variable: analysis-only cohort attribution; no executable policy changed

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull where accepted stack now beats indexes after recent allocation work
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape; still the weakest EV and win-rate window

weak_but_too_small_for_rule: breakout Communication Services gap/near-high overlap: 2 trades, -$110.10
cohort_findings.interpretation: The remaining negative pockets are mostly tiny reduced-risk cohorts. Promoting a new rule from 1-3 trades would violate the anti-overfit guardrail and is unlikely to clear Gate 4 materiality.

gate4_basis: No strategy-affecting change was promoted. The audit explicitly keeps fixed-window metrics unchanged and records why no weak cohort qualified for a production rule.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft-ranking data remains insufficient; this deterministic audit avoids depending on that blocked alpha source.

decision_reason: Observed only: the strongest allocation sleeves are already accepted, and the remaining negative pockets lack sample size and materiality for a new alpha rule.
next_retry_requires: Do not add a zero-risk rule for breakout Financials DTE or similar tiny cohorts without forward evidence or an orthogonal state/event discriminator.
Prioritize a richer meta-allocation state map or forward/paper evidence over local sizing tweaks.
If a future rule is promoted, keep it in shared portfolio_engine or production_parity policy from the start.
